_meta.description: Reusable AI explanation templates consumed by the explanation layer. The explanation layer fills in the variables and never makes optimization decisions itself.

transport_recommendations.template: Taking the metro reduces estimated emissions by {{emission_reduction_percent}}% and costs {{cost_savings}} less than a solo cab, with only {{time_delta}} more travel time.
Pooling this ride cuts emissions by roughly {{emission_reduction_percent}}% while staying within {{time_delta}} of your fastest option.
This trip is under {{distance_km}} km. Walking or cycling avoids emissions entirely and often beats traffic during peak hours.
Speed is the priority here, so the fastest option is recommended. The greener alternative would have saved about {{emission_reduction_percent}}% emissions for {{time_delta}} more travel time.

food_delivery_recommendations.template: {{restaurant_name}} is within {{distance_km}} km and uses {{packaging_type}}. Choosing it over the long-distance option cuts delivery emissions by about {{emission_reduction_percent}}%.
Group your order with {{nearby_orders_count}} nearby orders in the next {{minutes}} minutes to lower delivery emissions by roughly {{emission_reduction_percent}}%.
Switching to {{eco_packaging_type}} for this order reduces single-use plastic by about {{plastic_reduction_grams}} g without changing delivery time.
A {{diet_type}} version of {{dish_name}} is available from the same kitchen and lowers the meal's footprint by an estimated {{emission_reduction_percent}}%.

delivery_logistics_recommendations.template: You have {{open_orders_count}} open orders. Consolidating them into one shipment can reduce packaging waste by about {{waste_reduction_percent}}% and emissions by {{emission_reduction_percent}}%.
Switching from express to a {{eco_window_label}} window allows route batching and saves an estimated {{emission_reduction_percent}}% emissions on this delivery.
An electric delivery option is available for this route. It produces about {{co2_reduction_percent}}% less CO2 than the standard fleet at the same price.

tradeoff_explanations.template: The greenest option scores {{green_score}}/100 on sustainability but loses on {{losing_dimension}}. The balanced pick scores {{balanced_score}}/100 overall and stays within your {{constraint_label}}.
Accepting {{time_delta}} extra time and {{cost_delta}} lower cost yields a {{emission_reduction_percent}}% drop in emissions. Strong tradeoff on the balanced profile.
The top three options are within {{score_spread}} points of each other. Pick based on what matters most right now: {{dimension_a}}, {{dimension_b}}, or {{dimension_c}}.

sustainability_nudges.template: You've made {{eco_choice_count}} sustainable choices this week, saving an estimated {{co2_saved_kg}} kg of CO2. Keep the streak going.
If {{user_count}} people in your city made the same choice today, the city would avoid roughly {{co2_saved_tons}} tons of CO2.
SustainRoute compares your options across sustainability, cost, time, and convenience, then recommends the best balance. No tradeoffs forced.
You're {{progress_percent}}% toward your monthly goal of {{goal_value}} {{goal_unit}}. Today's recommendation contributes {{contribution_value}} {{goal_unit}}.

fallback.template: Based on sustainability, cost, time, and convenience, the best balanced option is {{recommended_option}}.
